summary: A solid soundboard capture of the 4/4/91 Omni show, highlighted by a smoking Help‑Slip‑Frank sequence and strong Garcia work.
review: The recording is generally clear – a soundboard source that most listeners describe as terrific, with only one note about cassette‑copy hiss. Musically the concert shines, especially the second set where the Help > Slip > Frank medley, Memphis blues jam, and tight first‑set opener receive high praise; Garcia’s lead work is called "strong" throughout and Phil’s bass is notably forward on the highlight songs.

ai_rating.rationale: The majority of reviews are 4‑5 stars, praising both performance (especially the second set) and recording quality; only a single low‑rating comment notes minor tape hiss, so overall rating is strong.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally clear and well‑balanced soundboard mix; one reviewer mentions the source being a cassette‑to‑cassette copy with noticeable hiss, but overall listeners find it terrific.

standout_songs: Help on the Way > Slipknot! > Franklin's Tower
Let The Good Times Roll / Good Times (Sam Cooke)
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set described as solid and fun, second set regarded as the highlight with seamless transitions and high energy.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s playing is called strong throughout, with standout leads on the second‑set jams.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass was turned up for the Help/Slip segment; one reviewer met Phil that night and highlighted his performance.
band_member_comments.Keys: Hornsby’s piano work is praised for adding richness to the mix, especially on the opener.
